zh.lifeSummary: 當Anna Sidney Lady Sutton Coldfield Warwickshire於1404年在英國素可列維斯列維斯 聖 多馬 亞特 克利夫受洗時，她的父親William Sidney II49歲，母親Alicia Clumford49歲。她於1430年與Sir Walter Harman在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾塞頓 可爾德費爾德結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世在英國瓦維其可爾塞頓 可爾德費爾德，埋葬在英國瓦維其可爾塞頓 可爾德費爾德霍利 特利尼迪 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Anna Sidney Lady Sutton Coldfield Warwickshire于1404年在英国素可列維斯列維斯 聖 多馬 亞特 克利夫受洗时，她的父亲William Sidney II49岁，母亲Alicia Clumford49岁。她于1430年与Sir Walter Harman在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾塞頓 可爾德費爾德结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世在英国瓦維其可爾塞頓 可爾德費爾德，埋葬在英国瓦維其可爾塞頓 可爾德費爾德霍利 特利尼迪 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Sidney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from Sidney Farm in Alfold (Surrey), from Old English sīd ‘large, spacious, long’ (dative sīdan) + ēg ‘island’.
possibly also a habitational name from any of the places called Saint-Denis in Normandy, named from the dedication of their churches to Saint Dionysius (see   Dennis  ).

variants.name: Dennis
meanings: English: from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Denis(e), Dinis(e), vernacular forms of Latin Dionysius, Greek Dionysios ‘(follower) of Dionysos’, an eastern god introduced to the classical pantheon at a relatively late date and bearing a name of probably Semitic origin. The name was borne by various early Christian saints, including Saint Denis, the martyred 3rd-century bishop of Paris who became the patron saint of France. From the 12th century onward, the popularity of the name in England (and of its cognates, mainly Denis , in the continental Europe) seems to have been largely due to French influence. The feminine form Dionysia (in vernacular English likewise Den(n)is) is also found, and some examples of the surname may originate from it.
English: from Middle English deneis, a variant of danais ‘Danish’, with the vowel influenced by Middle English denshe (Old English denisc). Compare Dence .

Irish (mainly Dublin and Cork): of the same origin as 1 and 2 above, sometimes an alternative form to Donohue but more often to McDonough , since the personal name Donnchadh was Anglicized as Donough or Denis.

variants.name: Sydney
meanings: English and Scottish (Midlothian): variant of   Sidney  .

nameMeanings.name: Anna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

variants.name: Anne
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Anna Sidney Lady Sutton Coldfield Warwickshire was christened in 1404, in Lewes St Thomas at Cliffe, Lewes, Sussex, England, her father, William Sidney II, was 49 and her mother, Alicia Clumford, was 49. She married Sir Walter Harman in 1430, in Sutton Coldfield, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died in Sutton Coldfield, Warwickshire, England, and was buried in Holy Trinity Church, Sutton Coldfield, Warwickshire, England.

Elle a épousé Sir Walter Harman en 1430, à Sutton Coldfield, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée à Sutton Coldfield, Warwickshire, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Holy Trinity Church, Sutton Coldfield, Warwickshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Anna Sidney Lady Sutton Coldfield Warwickshireが1404年にイギリススセックスルスルス スト ソマス アト クリフで洗礼を受けた時，父William Sidney IIは49歳，母Alicia Clumfordは49歳であった。1430年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアスットオン コルドフィールドでSir Walter Harmanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女はイギリスワーウィクシュアイアスットオン コルドフィールドで死去し，イギリスワーウィクシュアイアスットオン コルドフィールドホーリー トリニトイ チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Anna Sidney Lady Sutton Coldfield Warwickshire(이)가 1404년 영국 수세스 루스 루스 스트 톰아스 앳 클리프에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Sidney II(은)는 49세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alicia Clumford(은)는 49세였다. 그녀는 1430년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 수턴 콜드필드에서 Sir Walter Harman(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 워윅실르 수턴 콜드필드에서 사망했으며 영국 워윅실르 수턴 콜드필드 홀이 트리니티 c후르치에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Anna Sidney Lady Sutton Coldfield Warwickshire была крещена в 1404 г. в местности львесь ст томас ат клифф, львесь, суссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени William Sidney II было 49 лет, а матери, которую звали Alicia Clumford, 49. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Walter Harman в 1430 г. в местности суттон колдфиелд, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла в местности суттон колдфиелд, варвиккшир, Англия и была похоронена в местности холи тринити чурч, суттон колдфиелд, варвиккшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Anna Sidney Lady Sutton Coldfield Warwickshire se bautizó en 1404, en Lewes St Thomas at Cliffe, Lewes, Sussex, Inglaterra, su papá, William Sidney II, tenía 49 años y su mamá, Alicia Clumford, tenía 49 años. Se casó con Sir Walter Harman en 1430, en Sutton Coldfield, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió en Sutton Coldfield, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, y fue sepultada en Holy Trinity Church, Sutton Coldfield, Warwickshire, Inglaterra.
